name: Vegetable Saute with Fresh Peaches
recipeIngredient: 1 pinch lemon grass, dried OR 1/2 teaspoon lemon zest
1 slice gingerroot, fresh, 1-inch long, peeled and grated
1/4 cup mushrooms (shiitake), soaked in 1/2 cup warm water for 30 minutes and drained
recipeInstructions:
Sauce: Combine all sauce ingredients except water in a bowl. Stir or whisk until blended. Thin with hot water until the mixture has the consistency of ketchup.
Vegetables and rice: Bring water to boil in a saucepan. Add peaches. After water resumes boiling, wait 45 seconds, then remove peaches with a slotted spoon. Set aside to cool.
Peel peaches, slice them in half, and remove pits. Mash into pulp. Heat oil in a wok or heavy skillet. Add scallions, garlic, and ginger, and stir-fry for 2 minutes to flavor the oil. Add peach pulp, shiitake mushrooms, tofu, broccoli, and bean sprouts to the wok. Stir thoroughly, add a splash of rice vinegar, and keep stirring until it evaporates, about 1 minute. Add sauce and keep stirring until vegetables are evenly coated. Continue cooking, stirring constantly, until broccoli is bright green. Serve over cooked rice. Serves 4.
Per serving: 439 cal; 15 g prot; 16 g fat; 56 g carb; 0 chol; 353 mg sod; 8 g fiber
Vegetarian Times August 1993/MM by Deanne
